actions.name: Bite
actions.desc: Melee Attack: +10, reach 10 ft. 33 (4d12 + 7) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 17). While Grappled, the target has the Restrained condition and can't be targeted by the tyrannosaurus's Tail.

actions.name: Tail
actions.desc: Melee Attack: +10, reach 15 ft. 25 (4d8 + 7) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

monsters.name: Unicorn

special_abilities.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
special_abilities.desc: If the unicorn fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

special_abilities.name: Magic Resistance
special_abilities.desc: The unicorn has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

special_abilities.name: Spellcasting
special_abilities.desc:
The unicorn casts one of the following spells, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14):
At will: Detect Evil and Good, Druidcraft
1/day each: Calm Emotions, Dispel Evil and Good, Entangle, Pass without Trace, Word of Recall

special_abilities.name: Unicorn's Blessing (3/Day)
special_abilities.desc:
The unicorn touches another creature with its horn and casts Cure Wounds or Lesser Restoration on that creature, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.
3/day: Cure Wounds, Lesser Restoration

actions.name: Multiattack
actions.desc: The unicorn makes one Hooves attack and one Radiant Horn attack.

actions.name: Hooves
actions.desc: Melee Attack: +7, reach 5 ft. 11 (2d6 + 4) Bludgeoning damage.

actions.name: Radiant Horn
actions.desc: Melee Attack: +7, reach 5 ft. 9 (1d10 + 4) Radiant damage.

legendary_actions.name: Charging Horn
legendary_actions.desc: The unicorn moves up to half its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attack, and it makes one Radiant Horn attack.

legendary_actions.name: Shimmering Shield
legendary_actions.desc: The unicorn targets itself or one creature it can see within 60 feet of itself. The target gains 10 (3d6) Temporary Hit Points, and its AC increases by 2 until the end of the unicorn's next turn. The unicorn can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

monsters.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the unicorn can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the unicorn can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Shimmer of Protection
lair_actions.desc: The unicorn causes shimmering lights to appear in a 30-foot radius centered on a point it can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature of the unicorn's choice in that area gains 10 temporary hit points.

lair_actions.name: Healing Spring
lair_actions.desc: The unicorn targets one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. A shimmering spring of healing energy wells up beneath the target, restoring 10 (3d6) hit points to the target and ending one disease or one of the following conditions: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, or poisoned.

lair_actions.name: Calm Emotions
lair_actions.desc: The unicorn casts calm emotions with a DC 15 saving throw. The spell doesn't require concentration when cast this way.

monsters.regional_effects_intro:
A unicorn's presence sanctifies its forest home. The following magical effects are linked to the unicorn's lair:

If the unicorn dies, these effects fade over the course of 1d10 days.

regional_effects.name: Memory Loss
regional_effects.desc: Open flames of a nonmagical nature are extinguished within the unicorn's domain. Torches and campfires refuse to burn, but closed lanterns are unaffected.

regional_effects.name: No Tracking
regional_effects.desc: Creatures native to the unicorn's domain have an easier time hiding; they have advantage on all Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

regional_effects.name: Endless Sustenance
regional_effects.desc: When a good-aligned creature casts a spell or uses a magical effect that causes another good-aligned creature to regain hit points, the target regains the maximum number of hit points possible for the spell or effect.

monsters.name: Vampire

special_abilities.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day, or 4/Day in Lair)
special_abilities.desc: If the vampire fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

special_abilities.name: Misty Escape
special_abilities.desc:
If the vampire drops to 0 Hit Points outside its resting place, the vampire uses Shape-Shift to become mist (no action required). If it can't use Shape-Shift, it is destroyed.
While it has 0 Hit Points in mist form, it can't return to its vampire form, and it must reach its resting place within 2 hours or be destroyed. Once in its resting place, it returns to its vampire form and has the Paralyzed condition until it regains any Hit Points, and it regains 1 Hit Point after spending 1 hour there.

special_abilities.name: Spider Climb
special_abilities.desc: The vampire can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

special_abilities.name: Vampire Weakness
special_abilities.desc:
The vampire has these weaknesses:
- Forbiddance: The vampire can't enter a residence without an invitation from an occupant.
- Running Water: The vampire takes 20 Acid damage if it ends its turn in running water.
- Stake to the Heart: If a weapon that deals Piercing damage is driven into the vampire's heart while the vampire has the Incapacitated condition in its resting place, the vampire has the Paralyzed condition until the weapon is removed.
- Sunlight: The vampire takes 20 Radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

special_abilities.name: Charm (Recharge 5-6)
special_abilities.desc: The vampire casts Charm Person, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17), and the duration is 24 hours. The Charmed target is a willing recipient of the vampire's Bite, the damage of which doesn't end the spell. When the spell ends, the target is unaware it was Charmed by the vampire.

special_abilities.name: Beguile
special_abilities.desc: The vampire casts Command, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17). The vampire can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

actions.name: Multiattack (Vampire Form Only)
actions.desc: The vampire makes two Grave Strike attacks and uses Bite.

actions.name: Grave Strike (Vampire Form Only)
actions.desc: Melee Attack: +9, reach 5 ft. 8 (1d8 + 4) Bludgeoning damage plus 7 (2d6) Necrotic damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 14) from one of two hands.

actions.name: Bite (Bat or Vampire Form Only)
actions.desc: Constitution Saving Throw: DC 17, one creature within 5 feet that is willing or that has the Grappled, Incapacitated, or Restrained condition. Failure: 6 (1d4 + 4) Piercing damage plus 13 (3d8) Necrotic damage. The target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken, and the vampire regains Hit Points equal to that amount. A Humanoid reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage and then buried rises the following sunset as a Vampire Spawn under the vampire's control.

legendary_actions.name: Deathless Strike
legendary_actions.desc: The vampire moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Grave Strike attack.

monsters.regional_effects_intro:
The region surrounding a vampire's lair is warped by the creature's unnatural presence, creating any of the following effects:

If the vampire is destroyed, these effects end after 2d6 days.

regional_effects.name: Vermin Surge
regional_effects.desc: There's a noticeable increase in the populations of bats, rats, and wolves in the region.

regional_effects.name: Withered Plants
regional_effects.desc: Plants within 500 feet of the lair wither, and their stems and branches become twisted and thorny.

regional_effects.name: Living Shadows
regional_effects.desc: Shadows cast within 500 feet of the lair seem abnormally gaunt and sometimes move as though alive.

regional_effects.name: Creeping Fog
regional_effects.desc: A creeping fog clings to the ground within 500 feet of the vampire's lair. The fog occasionally takes eerie forms, such as grasping claws and writhing serpents.

- Forbiddance: The vampire can't enter a residence without an invitation from an occupant.
- Running Water: The vampire takes 20 Acid damage if it ends its turn in running water.
- Stake to the Heart: The vampire is destroyed if a weapon that deals Piercing damage is driven into the vampire's heart while the vampire has the Incapacitated condition.
- Sunlight: The vampire takes 20 Radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
